title: AI Agent Fundamentals
milestone: Understand what an agent is and how to reason about input, process, and output.
summary: What an agent is, what it is not, and how to reason about input/output without jargon.

title: Skills and Responsibilities
milestone: Distinguish roles and avoid mixing planning, QA, and development.
summary: How to separate planning, development, testing, and review to reduce errors.

title: Full Template Workflow
milestone: Walk through the official workflow from idea to delivery with traceability.
summary: Path from planner → qa-strategy → dev → reviewer using real repository examples.

title: Skill Lab I (00, 10, 20)
milestone: Master profile, planning, and safe changes before implementing complex features.
summary: Granular explanation of 00-project-profile, 10-plan-first, and 20-safe-changes with practical examples.

title: Skill Lab II (30, 40)
milestone: Apply structured review and testing strategy to close the quality cycle.
summary: Detailed explanation of 30-code-review and 40-tests to validate delivery with confidence.

title: Meta Session · How chat interprets .md files
milestone: Understand how AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, and instructions guide agent behavior.
summary: Practical explanation of how chat prioritizes and applies markdown rules in the workflow.

title: .agents architecture (roles and skills)
milestone: Understand how conceptual agents and skills are organized in the .agents layer.
summary: Walk through .agents/*.agent.md, AGENT_MAPPING, and skills to explain purpose, dependencies, and practical usage.

title: .codex architecture (runtime execution)
milestone: Distinguish conceptual vs runtime layers and the role of builder/planner/qa/reviewer/security.
summary: Explains .codex/agents/*.toml as runtime configuration and its connection to .agents intent.

title: Root markdowns as governance system
milestone: Apply AGENTS.md, AGENTS_CHANGELOG, AI_WORKFLOW, and SYSTEM_OVERVIEW as a stable decision framework.
summary: Integrated session on root repository documents and how to preserve traceability, change control, and workflow safety.
